RDB INFRASTRUCTURE ENTERS SOLAR CELL MANUFACTURING SPACE ☀️🔋

• 🏭 RDB Infrastructure & Power Ltd has invested in newly incorporated Maxim Industries Pvt Ltd

• 💰 Company subscribed to 43,500 equity shares for ₹4.35 lakh

• 📊 Investment will give RDB an approximately 29% stake in Maxim Industries

• ☀️ Maxim Industries will operate in the Solar Cell Manufacturing segment

• 🇮🇳 Investment aims to strengthen RDB's participation in India's growing renewable energy sector

• 🆕 Maxim Industries was incorporated on 2 June 2026 and is currently at a nascent stage

• 🔋 Move provides RDB an entry into the solar manufacturing value chain amid increasing domestic solar capacity expansion

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic entry into solar cell manufacturing aligns with India's renewable energy growth theme and diversifies RDB's business portfolio, though the investment size is currently small.)

reports.text:
HFCL BUILDS INTEGRATED DEFENCE PLATFORM; ACQUIRES AEROSTRUCTURE BUSINESS ✈️🛡️

• 🎯 HASPL (HFCL Advance Systems) to acquire 100% stake in HDSPL from Defsys group for ₹25 Cr

• 🏭 HDSPL will acquire the Aerostructure Business of Defsys on a slump-sale basis for ₹25 Cr

• 📦 Transaction provides immediate access to an export order book of ~₹1,890 Cr

• ✈️ Strengthens HFCL's presence in the fast-growing aerostructures and aerospace manufacturing segment

• 🔧 Brings established manufacturing capabilities, certifications, customer relationships and defence credentials

• 🛡️ Combines HFCL's existing strengths in radar & surveillance systems, thermal weapon sights and defence electronics with aerostructure capabilities

• 🚀 Creates a focused and scalable defence platform under HASPL for future growth

• 🇮🇳 Expected to enhance participation in defence procurement programs and support the Make in India initiative

• 🌍 Operating in a high-entry-barrier sector, the acquisition significantly strengthens HFCL's domestic and export opportunities

🟢 Impact: Strong Positive (HFCL is transforming HASPL into a full-fledged defence and aerospace platform. The acquisition adds a sizeable ₹1,890 Cr export order book, expands capabilities across the defence value chain and improves long-term growth visibility.)

HFCL RESTRUCTURES DEFENCE BUSINESS; TRANSFERS TWS BUSINESS TO HASPL 🛡️📡

• 🔄 HFCL will transfer its Thermal Weapon Sight (TWS) business to HFCL Advance Systems Pvt Ltd (HASPL)

• 💰 Transfer will be executed on a slump sale basis for a lump-sum consideration of ₹50 Cr

• 🎯 Move is part of HFCL's strategy to consolidate defence and aerospace businesses under a dedicated defence platform

• 🏭 HASPL is being positioned as HFCL's integrated defence subsidiary covering aerospace, aerostructures, radar, surveillance and weapon systems

• 📈 The restructuring is expected to improve operational focus, scalability and execution capabilities in the defence segment

• 🤝 Transaction will be carried out through a Business Transfer Agreement (BTA), subject to completion of conditions precedent

• 🚀 Supports HFCL's long-term ambition to build a focused defence and aerospace platform with stronger technology and manufacturing capabilities

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic business realignment strengthens HFCL's defence vertical, improves organizational focus and could unlock higher valuation for its defence business over time.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
HFCL TO MONETIZE RADDEF AT ₹75 CR VALUATION 💰🛡️

• 🔄 HFCL will sell up to 80% stake in Raddef Pvt Ltd to its defence subsidiary HFCL Advance Systems Pvt Ltd (HASPL)

• 💰 HFCL will receive ₹75 Cr as consideration under the Share Purchase Agreement (SPA)

• 📊 Raddef generated ₹9.04 Cr revenue in FY26, contributing only 0.18% of HFCL's consolidated revenue

• 📉 Raddef reported a negative net worth of ₹2.32 Cr as of March 31, 2026

• 🏭 Buyer HASPL is a wholly-owned subsidiary being developed as HFCL's integrated defence platform

• 🎯 Transaction is aimed at consolidating defence assets and capabilities under HASPL

• 📅 Deal is expected to be completed during CY2026, subject to conditions under the SPA

• 🤝 Related-party transaction, but being executed on an arm's-length basis

🟢 Impact: Positive (HFCL is effectively unlocking ₹75 Cr value from a loss-making, non-core subsidiary while streamlining and strengthening its defence business structure under HASPL.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
HFCL FORMS SHAREHOLDERS' AGREEMENT FOR DEFENCE SUBSIDIARY HASPL 🛡️🤝

• 📝 HFCL, HASPL, Anant Nahata, Sushant Mohan Gupta, Shubhra Gupta, BAS Engineering and ITI Holdings have signed a Shareholders' Agreement (SHA)

• 🎯 The agreement will govern management, administration, governance, board composition and shareholder rights of HFCL Advance Systems Pvt Ltd (HASPL)

• 🏭 HASPL is being developed as HFCL's dedicated defence & security platform

• 👥 Post transaction, HFCL Group will hold 51.02% stake, retaining majority control

• 🏛️ HASPL's Board will comprise 6 directors, with HFCL Group having the right to nominate 4 directors and DMG Group 2 directors

• 🔒 Agreement includes provisions for veto rights, pre-emptive rights, ROFR, tag-along, drag-along rights and transfer restrictions

• 💰 HASPL will issue 1.75 crore equity shares aggregating ₹175 Cr to incoming investors under the share subscription agreement

• 📈 The structure provides strategic capital, governance framework and long-term alignment among shareholders

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens institutional framework for HFCL's defence business, enables capital infusion while retaining control, and supports scaling of the defence platform.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
HFCL TO INVEST ₹89.25 CR IN DEFENCE ARM HASPL 🚀🛡️

• 🏭 HFCL will invest ₹89.25 Cr in its defence subsidiary HFCL Advance Systems Pvt Ltd (HASPL) through equity subscription in two tranches

• 🎯 HASPL is being positioned as HFCL's integrated defence platform covering aerospace, aerostructures, radar & surveillance systems, and thermal weapon sights

• 🤝 Strategic investors including Anant Nahata, Sushant Mohan Gupta, Shubhra Gupta, BAS Engineering and ITI Holdings will collectively invest ₹175 Cr

• 📈 Post transaction, HFCL will retain 51.02% stake in HASPL, ensuring continued control

• 🛡️ The investment aims to strengthen HFCL's presence in the rapidly growing defence and security sector

• 🌍 Management expects the platform to enhance competitiveness in both domestic and global defence markets

• 📅 Transaction is expected to be completed during CY2026, subject to conditions under the Share Subscription Agreement

🟢 Impact: Positive (Creates a dedicated defence growth platform, attracts strategic capital, and unlocks value in HFCL's defence business while retaining majority control.)

reports.text:
PRAVEG GETS GST CASE CLOSED WITH ZERO LIABILITY ✅

• ⚖️ GST authorities have disposed of proceedings against Praveg Limited related to an earlier Show Cause Notice

• 📅 The notice was issued in July 2025 alleging wrongful availment of Input Tax Credit (ITC) linked to construction of immovable property

• 💰 Earlier disclosed potential financial implication was ₹2.47 Cr

• 🟢 Assistant Commissioner, Vadodara Commissionerate, passed an order on June 3, 2026, concluding the proceedings

• ✅ No tax demand, interest, penalty or any other liability has been imposed on the company

• 📌 Matter stands fully disposed of with nil financial impact

🟢 Impact: Positive (Removes a regulatory overhang and eliminates potential GST liability exposure.)

RAJESH EXPORTS UNDER SEBI SCANNER OVER ALLEGED REVENUE MISREPORTING 🚨⚖️

• 🔍 SEBI has flagged alleged ₹15.15 lakh crore revenue misrepresentation by Rajesh Exports, primarily linked to overseas subsidiaries

• 🌍 Nearly 98–99% of reported revenue over multiple years reportedly originated from foreign entities, raising disclosure concerns

• 📑 SEBI noted inadequate disclosure of key financial information related to overseas subsidiaries

• 🚫 The regulator alleged the company failed to provide transaction-level records and supporting evidence sought during the investigation

• ⚠️ SEBI's interim order cites prima facie violations of disclosure norms, corporate governance requirements and anti-fraud regulations

🔴 Impact: Negative (Raises serious governance and accounting concerns, potentially impacting investor confidence and regulatory scrutiny.)

✅ Expands Antara's presence in preventive and wellness healthcare

✅ Creates a new revenue stream beyond senior housing

✅ Offers specialised programs across JointCare, HeartCare, DiabetesCare, GutCare, MindCare, CancerCare and other chronic conditions

📈 India's senior population is expected to reach 347 million by 2050, creating a significant long-term opportunity in senior healthcare.

JAIN IRRIGATION COMMISSIONS WORLD-CLASS BIOCHAR FACILITY 🌱♻️

• 🚀 Jain Irrigation has commissioned an industrial-scale biochar facility in Jalgaon, among the world's largest single-unit biochar reactors

• 🌾 The plant can process 20,000+ tonnes of agricultural residue annually and has a capacity of 50+ TPD

• 🌍 Biochar production helps generate durable carbon removal credits (CDR) while improving soil fertility and water retention

• 👨‍🌾 The initiative supports over 10 million farmers through a farm-to-soil circular economy model

• 🇮🇳 The Jalgaon facility is the first of multiple planned reactors, positioning India as a leader in biochar and carbon removal technologies

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Jain Irrigation's sustainability leadership, opens carbon credit opportunities, and creates a new growth avenue in climate-smart agriculture.)

Yardeni Research, the most bullish Wall Street forecaster with an 8,250 S&P 500 target, says it is turning cautious in the near term despite the index topping 7,600.

• 📈 Management expects TheraNym to strengthen India's position in biologics manufacturing and expand Aurobindo's presence in the global CDMO market

• 🔬 Facility aims to support development and commercial manufacturing of complex biologic medicines for global pharmaceutical companies

🟢 Impact: Positive (TheraNym expands Aurobindo's biologics capabilities, adds a global anchor customer in MSD, and opens a high-growth CDMO revenue opportunity in the fast-expanding biologics market.)

reports.text:
DELPHI WORLD MONEY GETS COURT RELIEF IN DWARKA COMMERCIAL COURT CASE ⚖️✅

• 🏛️ Dwarka Commercial Court, New Delhi has returned the plaint filed by Bull Value Incorporated VCC Sub-Fund and related parties

• 📌 Earlier status quo directions against Delphi have been vacated

• 📄 Court noted that despite being given two opportunities, the plaintiff failed to produce FCCB title documents supporting its claim as a bondholder

• 🔍 Court observations questioned the factual basis on which proceedings against the company were initiated

• 🛡️ Delphi stated that it had consistently maintained that the allegations and proceedings were misconceived and unsupported by evidence

• 🤝 Company said the litigation had created uncertainty among shareholders, customers, lenders and other stakeholders

• 🚀 Delphi reiterated that business operations continue normally and remains focused on: • Expanding business opportunities • Strengthening governance and compliance • Protecting stakeholder interests • Creating long-term value

• 📈 Management believes the court order reaffirms that serious allegations against businesses should be supported by credible evidence

🟢 Impact: Positive (Vacating of status quo orders and adverse observations against the plaintiff strengthen Delphi's legal position, reduce uncertainty and improve confidence among stakeholders.)

• 📅 Facility went live on June 1, 2026

• 🎯 Enhances customer convenience with a secure, efficient and user-friendly payment experience

• 🏛️ Strengthens Karnataka Bank's Government Business portfolio

• 🚀 Supports the bank's ongoing digital transformation initiatives and improves transaction efficiency

🟢 Impact: Positive (EPFO integration strengthens Karnataka Bank's digital banking ecosystem, enhances customer convenience and can support growth in transaction volumes and government-related banking business.)

reports.text:
INSPIRISYS RESOLVES KMSCL DISPUTE; COURT DISPOSES CASE 🤝⚖️

• ✅ Long-pending litigation between Inspirisys Solutions and Kerala Medical Services Corporation Limited (KMSCL) has been amicably settled through mediation

• 🏛️ Hon'ble High Court of Kerala at Ernakulam approved the mediation settlement and disposed of the matter on April 10, 2026

• 📜 Certified copy of the court order was received by the company on June 3, 2026

• 💰 Demand of ₹75.89 lakh raised against the company now stands resolved and disposed of

• 🤝 As part of the settlement, the company paid ₹76.83 lakh towards the bank guarantee

• ✨ All claims and counterclaims between both parties have been mutually waived and fully settled

• 🔚 The dispute originated from services rendered by the company to KMSCL under a 2011 tender contract

🟢 Impact: Positive (Resolution of a long-standing legal dispute removes uncertainty, closes contingent liabilities, and improves business visibility without any further litigation overhang.)

reports.text:
ZYDUS LIFESCIENCES RECEIVES USFDA WARNING LETTER FOR BADDI FACILITY ⚠️💊

• 🇺🇸 Received USFDA Warning Letter for its formulations manufacturing facility at Baddi, Himachal Pradesh

• 📄 Warning Letter relates to a records request under Section 704(a)(4) of the US Federal Food, Drug & Cosmetic Act

• 🚰 Communication cites technical observations regarding purified water usage that did not meet certain USP requirements

• 🏭 Letter does not pertain to any on-site USFDA inspection

• ✅ Company stated the Warning Letter is not expected to impact current operations or supplies from the Baddi facility

• 🔍 Baddi plant underwent a USFDA inspection in August 2025

• 📋 Company received the Establishment Inspection Report (EIR) in October 2025 with final compliance status classified as VAI (Voluntary Action Indicated)

• 🤝 Zydus said it will work closely with USFDA to complete remediation measures within stipulated timelines

🔴 Impact: Negative (Receipt of a USFDA Warning Letter is a regulatory concern and may weigh on sentiment, although the company maintains there will be no impact on current operations and supplies.)

NMDC increases iron ore prices by INR 150-200/t for Jun'26

India's largest merchant iron ore mining company, NMDC, has increased its list prices of iron ore CLO (calibrated lump ore) and fines on 6 June 2026, BigMint learnt from sources. 

The miner has fixed prices of DR CLO (10-40 mm, Fe 67%) at INR 6,150/tonne (t) ($65/t) and of iron ore fines (-10 mm, Fe 64%) at INR 4,700/t ($49.5/t). Prices are on FOR basis from the miner's Bacheli complex and exclude royalty, DMF, and NMEDT. Prices of all grades rose in the range of INR 50-100/t.

SHILPA MEDICARE PARTNERS WITH SPAIN'S GATE2BRAIN FOR BRAIN CANCER THERAPY 🧠💊

• 🤝 Shilpa Biocare (wholly-owned subsidiary) to acquire 30.4% stake in Spain-based Gate2Brain

• 💶 Total investment of €7 million (~₹69 Cr)

• 💰 Structure: €0.5 mn cash infusion, €5.5 mn equity for services, €1 mn for project development

• 🎯 Partnership focused on G2B-002, a first-in-class brain cancer therapy using blood-brain barrier (BBB) penetrating peptide-shuttle technology

• 🏥 Shilpa becomes strategic shareholder and integrated CMC, manufacturing & regulatory partner

• 🌍 G2B-002 has received Orphan Drug Designation (ODD) from both US FDA and EMA

• 🧬 Targets high-unmet-need brain cancers including Glioblastoma (GBM) and Diffuse Intrinsic Pontine Glioma (DIPG)

• 📈 Preclinical studies showed up to 100x higher brain drug transport compared to conventional approaches

• 🚀 Clinical batches expected next year; first-in-human trials targeted by FY28

• 💹 Addressable markets:

Glioblastoma market projected to reach $6.48 bn by 2035

Pediatric brain tumor market projected at $2.47 bn by 2031

DIPG market projected at $1.2 bn by 2035

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Shilpa's innovative oncology pipeline, expands presence in high-value CNS therapeutics, and creates long-term value through strategic ownership in a differentiated drug platform.)

Supreme Power Equipment: Guidance -- Concall Update 

Management targets FY27 revenue between INR 275 crore to INR 300 crore, with an additional INR 100 crore expected for FY28.

The company expects to execute INR 275 crore to INR 300 crore of its robust INR 588.17 crore order book in FY27, providing strong revenue visibility.

PAT margins are expected to be maintained at 10% to 12%, with a slight 1-2% higher margin on larger transformers being offset by increased skilled labor costs.

The 14.6% EBITDA margin reported in H2 FY26 is expected to be sustainable for Q1 FY27, as most raw materials were booked in March, mitigating price escalation risks.

The new manufacturing facility is projected to reach its optimal utilization of approximately 90% within two to three years.

Steel Strips Wheels: Guidance - - Concall Update 

Management expects a PAT growth of 15% to 20% for the current year (FY27), based on flourishing domestic and export segments.

The company projects an EBITDA per wheel of close to INR 300 for the current year (FY27), up from INR 272 in FY26, and potentially another 10% higher in FY28.

Total EBITDA is projected to be close to INR 650 crores for FY27, with an upward bias, and could reach INR 700 crores to INR 750 crores in FY28.

Export revenue is expected to be around INR 600 crores for FY27, which would be the second highest ever, driven by rationalized tariffs and diversified market access.

The company aims for almost 100% utilization of all commissioned assets in FY27, specifically targeting 95% utilization for steel plants.

The new Bhuj facility, with a CapEx of INR 500 crores for aluminum wheels (1.2 million units) and knuckles (1.1 million units), is expected to add INR 700-800 crores in revenue at full utilization. Trial production will begin from October and culminate in January, with significant utilization (70% or higher) expected in FY28.

Debt is projected to increase by approximately INR 200 crores in FY27 due to CapEx, with a review planned for September.

The EV segment (scooters and 3-wheelers) is expected to grow significantly, potentially 25% to 40% for the full year, with the company being a near-monopoly supplier for EV scooter wheels.

Brownfield expansion in steel wheels will increase tractor capacity by 15% to 20% OTR, with new paint shops and rim lines coming online around October to December.

Titagarh Rail System: Guidance -- Concall Update 

Management expects the passenger rail system business to scale up significantly, targeting 200 cars in FY27, up from 63 cars in FY26.

The long-term margin guidance for passenger rail systems remains around 12%, with potential for enhancement through backward integration and volume growth.

For the freight rail segment, management anticipates a run rate higher than last year, with sustained healthy demand for wagons due to India's economic growth and railway network expansion.

Freight business margins are expected to remain stable at approximately 12% on a steady-state basis, subject to mix of orders and commodity prices.

The wheelset joint venture is expected to start production in June (current quarter), with a ramp-up plan to fully achieve its 80,000 wheels per annum contract with the railways over 20 years.

Forest → Pulpwood harvesting → Chemical pulp mills (kraft process) → Kraft paper manufacturers → Lamination players

Key players globally: UPM, Sappi, Mondi, APP (Asia Pulp & Paper), JK Paper (India). These are large, capital-intensive businesses with significant pricing power during supply tightness. 

Kraft paper prices are linked to global pulp cycles when demand for packaging (cardboard, cartons) rises, pulp gets diverted, and laminate-grade kraft paper prices spike. Lamination players have zero influence on this cycle

Twelve deals. Five months. $92 million. 𓇲
​Indian semicon startups are scaling from Bengaluru labs to battling in Nvidia’s backyard.

2025 full year: Indian semicon startups raised ~$23-25M.

2026 (first 5 months): $94M raised already across 12 deals.

That’s a phase change. Indian semiconductor startups are driving cutting-edge developments. It also represents early success of Indian Govt's Design Linked Incentives (DLI) scheme.

• Agrani Labs is attacking the AI GPU + software stack. 
• C2I is solving the power wall in AI data centers. 
• Mindgrove and others are strengthening India’s SoC capabilities.

This is a strategy shift from cost arbitrage to proprietary IP + product-led companies, backed by DLI incentives and top-tier VCs.

Big implications:
- Reduced dependence on imports
- High-value job creation for semiconductor talent.
- Potential for India to become a relevant player in the global AI supply chain by 2030.

The India semiconductor story just went from “if” to “how fast.”

The US market for this medicine is worth $30 million, giving Concord a big revenue opportunity

The global aluminium supply crunch is getting worse.

What was expected to be a 1.5 MT deficit is now projected to widen to nearly 2 MT - the largest supply-demand imbalance in more than a decade.

The reason is simple: China, which accounts for roughly 60% of global aluminium production, has little room left to increase output.

Hydropower shortages, environmental restrictions, and production caps are preventing meaningful capacity additions just as global demand continues to rise.

Aluminium prices have already surged above $3,500/tonne, and if the deficit persists, higher prices could be here for longer than many expect.

Indian manufacturers could be among the biggest beneficiaries of a prolonged aluminium shortage.

Key listed players :

• Hindalco
• NALCO
• Vedanta

India remains one of the few major aluminium-producing nations with room to expand output while China faces production caps and power constraints.

Alphabet to raise $80B from share sales to fund AI spending splurge, Berkshire to invest $10B

Very bullish news, as it shows again the limiting factor is no longer demand, models, or products, but compute supply.

For years, the cleanest story was excess cash, buybacks, and software-like returns; now the story is closer to railroads, power grids, and fabs, where whoever can finance the bottleneck may own the toll road.

Berkshire’s $10B placement gives the raise a stamp of patient capital.

Alphabet is not simply selling $80B of common stock straight into the market tomorrow for AI capex. It is using a mix of underwritten stock, mandatory convertible preferred, Berkshire private placement, and a $40B ATM program, with about $30B of that ATM tied to employee equity tax mechanics.

Google on AI

“AI is driving an expansionary moment for Alphabet. The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply.”
In roughly 28 years, Google/Alphabet has tapped primary equity capital only seven times; the 2026 announced $80B raise is more than 10x all prior identified primary raises combined.

Alphabet to raise $80B from share sales to fund AI spending splurge, Berkshire to invest $10B

Very bullish news, as it shows again the limiting factor is no longer demand, models, or products, but compute supply.

For years, the cleanest story was excess cash, buybacks, and software-like returns; now the story is closer to railroads, power grids, and fabs, where whoever can finance the bottleneck may own the toll road.

Berkshire’s $10B placement gives the raise a stamp of patient capital.

Alphabet is not simply selling $80B of common stock straight into the market tomorrow for AI capex. It is using a mix of underwritten stock, mandatory convertible preferred, Berkshire private placement, and a $40B ATM program, with about $30B of that ATM tied to employee equity tax mechanics.

Uday Kotak (@udaykotak) posts, "Google which is cash surplus, just announced an additional capital raise of $80 bn. Google annual profit is $160 bn, last quarter $62 bn, and market cap $4.5 trillion. That is close to total profits and market cap of all Indian listed companies put together. It’s a wake up call to all companies  to invest into the future, whatever the present maybe. Now that IPL is done and dusted, time for India to focus on business of business."

Hindustan Zinc Ltd (HZL) on 1 June 2026 reduced zinc ingot prices by INR 3,300/t ($39/t) and lead ingot prices by INR 2,100/t ($25/t) compared with its previous revision announced on 28 May.

Special High Grade-Continuous Galvanizing Grade (SHG-CGG): INR 377,700/t

🏨 **SUBA HOTELS CROSSES 100 HOTEL MILESTONE, REPORTS RECORD FY26 REVENUE**

Suba Hotels delivered its highest-ever revenue of ₹115.89 Cr in FY26, registering 45% YoY growth while expanding its footprint to 102+ operational hotels across 73 destinations.

📈 Key Highlights

• Revenue: ₹115.89 Cr (+45% YoY)
• EBITDA: ₹26.82 Cr (+13% YoY)
• PAT: ₹18.01 Cr (+19% YoY)
• 102+ operational hotels and 4,660+ keys

💬 Management highlighted that revenue growth was driven by network expansion, higher business volumes, and strong traction across brands and operating formats.

⚠️ Despite strong operating performance, EBITDA and PAT growth lagged revenue growth due to changes in the GST framework during FY26. The loss of Input Tax Credit (ITC) benefits on certain operating expenses increased the industry's cost base and impacted profitability.

🎯 Looking ahead, the company remains focused on asset-light expansion, operational efficiencies, and strengthening its presence across high-growth hospitality markets.

**DATA CENTRE INDUSTRY – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**

**Industry Outlook**
- Decadal growth opportunity
- India DC industry CAGR: 30%
- CY25–30 growth outlook strong
- Expected to outperform APAC

**Key Demand Drivers**
- Digital adoption accelerating
- Cloud adoption expanding
- GenAI driving infrastructure demand
- Data consumption rising rapidly

**Industry Economics**
- Lower capex intensity supportive
- Favorable power economics
- Attractive project returns
- Premium pricing remains sustainable

**Equipment Opportunity**
- Strong demand visibility
- Benefiting industrial equipment suppliers
- Multi-year capex cycle emerging
- Large addressable market

**Key Product Categories**
- Switchgear and transformers
- UPS and battery systems
- Diesel and gas gensets
- Precision cooling systems
- Rack and cabling infrastructure

**Capex Intensity**
- DC capex: US$10–22 Mn/MW
- Equipment share: 60–75%
- Significant equipment opportunity
- High infrastructure spend required

**Competitive Landscape**
- Market remains consolidated
- Pricing discipline remains strong
- Oligopolistic industry structure
- Entry barriers remain high

**Key Beneficiaries**
- ABB India
- Siemens India
- Hitachi Energy
- GE Vernova T&D
- CG Power
- Cummins India

**Investment Takeaway**
- Structural multi-decade theme
- Strong demand visibility
- Premium pricing environment
- Attractive opportunity for equipment suppliers

**ZEE ENTERTAINMENT – CLSA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**

**Brokerage View**
- Outperform rating maintained
- Target price: ₹125

**FIFA Rights Win**
- Won rights for 39 FIFA events
- Includes FIFA World Cup 2026
- Includes FIFA World Cup 2030
- Rights secured for India market

**Sports Strategy**
- FIFA events for eight years
- Broadcast across sports channels
- Streaming via Zee5 platform
- Strengthens sports presence

**Revenue Opportunity**
- Boost to sports advertising
- Subscription revenue opportunity
- Expands non-cricket sports portfolio
- Enhances viewer engagement

**Economics of Deal**
- Rights reportedly below US$60 Mn
- Lower than FIFA 2022 valuation
- Attractive acquisition economics

**Sports Market Context**
- India sports revenue: ₹223 Bn
- Cricket accounts for 90% share
- FIFA offers diversification opportunity
- Global football audience attractive

**Financial Strength**
- Zee5 has reached breakeven
- Cash balance at ₹27.6 Bn
- Strong balance sheet position

**Valuation**
- Trading below 1x sales
- Trading at 12x FY27 earnings
- Valuation remains attractive

**Investment View**
- FIFA rights strengthen sports strategy
- Balance sheet remains strong
- Zee5 profitability improving
- Attractive valuation supports outlook

**RELIANCE INDUSTRIES – JPM REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**

**Brokerage View**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target price: ₹1,660

**Key Positives**
- Refining spreads remain strong
- Petrochemical spreads remain healthy
- Weaker INR supports earnings
- Valuations remain reasonable

**Growth Catalysts**
- New Energy commissioning ahead
- FY27 likely catalyst year
- Multiple growth engines operational

- Headline capex: ₹1.4 trillion
- Headline capex up 10% YoY
- Cash flow capex: ₹1.2 trillion

- Significant increase YoY

**Investment View**
- Refining cycle remains supportive
- Petrochemical environment improving
- New Energy remains key trigger
- Valuation comfort continues

reports.text:
Nomura On Data Centers

BROKERAGE RADAR

Decadal growth opportunity

Industry to record 30% CAGR over CY25-30F

India to outperform broader APAC

Lower capex and favourable power economics support attractive returns

India's digital, cloud and GenAl adoption driving demand

Attractive opportunities for industrial equipment manufacturers

Top picks: CGPOWER and Ge Vernova TD to benefit

**Must JOIN ****⬇️****
****@Stocksip**

Systematix On NMDC

BROKERAGE RADAR

Maintain BUY on NMDC with a revised target price of Rs 112

Strong FY26 execution with future growth trajectory intact

Management has guided for 60mt production in FY27

Management expects EBITDA margin to recover to ~35-40%

Raises FY27E/FY28E EBITDA estimates by 10%/8% and PAT estimates by 23%/21%

Revision is built on NMDC's strong execution track record

Jefferies On Cement

Mixed March-Quarter, Weak Pricing Follow Through Amid Cost Pressures

UltraTech outperformed again with strong delivery on both Vol/Unit EBITDA

Industry demand remained steady at ~7-7.5% year-on-year

Pricing up ~2% quarter-on-quarter and costs easing on Oplev

Jefferies Indian Pharma

March-Quarter Pharma Review: More Hits than Misses

Lupin and Zydus were strong while Dr Reddy and Cipla were weak

Raised EPS est for Zydus the most (14%) while Lupin saw 5-10% EPS cuts

Key monitorable going forward are cost pressures from prolonged West Asia conflict

Top picks: Mankind Pharma, Ajanta Pharma, Zydus Health

Morgan Stanley On India Equity Strategy

Bottom maybe behind us

With earnings growth acceleration likely in the pipeline and valuations and sentiment coming off near extremes

Indian equities appear poised for a strong year ahead

The long-term story is intact notwithstanding the Al debate

Domestic Cyclicals > Defensives and External-facing sectors; overweight Financials, Consumer Discretionary and Industrials

Morgan Stanley On India Equity Strategy

Underweight Energy, Materials, Utilities and Healthcare

IT services could be the dark horse as the world pivots to these companies to build Al applications and solutions

Key risks to India are mostly external, including geopolitical tensions and slowing global growth

Worry about the low productivity in farming, capacity constraints in the judiciary and embodied Al hitting the labour markets

**TILAKNAGAR INDUSTRIES – SYSTEMATIX REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**

**Brokerage View**
- BUY rating maintained
- Target price: ₹558
- CMP: ₹456
- Upside potential: 22%

**Investment Thesis**
- Imperial Blue acquisition transformational
- Pan-India liquor presence established
- Premiumisation driving growth
- Debt reduction remains focus

**Imperial Blue Integration**
- FY26 volume: 6.4 million cases
- 75% volumes exited TSMA
- Full transition by Mar'27
- Key synergy driver ahead

**Synergy Potential**
- Procurement savings expected
- Packaging costs to reduce
- Manufacturing optimization underway
- Logistics efficiencies improving
- Margin expansion: 250–400 bps

**Andhra Pradesh Expansion**
- Capacity increased 6x
- Capacity now 36 lakh cases
- Investment of ₹60 Cr
- Annual savings around ₹10 Cr

**Growth Outlook**
- FY27: high single-digit growth
- FY28–30: low-mid teens growth
- Revenue growth above volumes
- Premiumisation supporting realizations

**Margin Outlook**
- Q4 adjusted margin 15.5%
- FY29 target margin 16–18%
- TSMA exit to aid margins
- Operating leverage improving

**Karnataka Opportunity**
- Retail prices cut ₹20/nip
- Imperial Blue to benefit
- Mansion House to benefit
- Volume acceleration expected

**Premiumisation Focus**
- Monarch Brandy scaling up
- Amara Vodka expansion
- Luxury portfolio growing
- New launches planned

**Brandy Expansion**
- North India expansion planned
- Mansion House rollout
- Courrier Napoleon rollout
- Monarch rollout planned

**Debt Reduction**
- Net debt at ₹1,911 Cr
- FY27 target: ₹1,700 Cr
- Net debt/EBITDA below 1x

**Nigeria Business**
- ₹30 Cr investment approved
- Strategic long-term market
- Profitable operations maintained

**Financial Outlook**
- FY27 Revenue: ₹4,524 Cr
- FY28 Revenue: ₹4,977 Cr
- FY28 EBITDA: ₹788 Cr
- FY28 PAT: ₹363 Cr

**Key Risks**
- Integration execution risk
- Raw material inflation risk
- Maharashtra market challenges
- Elevated debt levels

**Key Monitorables**
- Complete TSMA exit
- Margin expansion delivery
- Faster debt reduction
- Distribution synergies realization
